title: Career Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Career Counseling 29 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Blindness Women ACCENT NETWORK IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support with resumes, job searches, credential evaluation, and digital literacy, along with career counseling and career planning guidance. Also offers financial wellness services, including workshops on saving, budgeting, and debt management, and one-on-one coaching. 12254 SW Garden Place Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with a focus on Russian and Ukrainian population Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-8pm Email (971) 280-6738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY CENTRO DE PROSPERIDAD HILLSBORO CAREER DEVELOPMENT AND SKILLS ASSESSMENT WORKSHOP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Agency offers help to prepare for interviews, build a resume, and provides educational and preparation courses such as: OSHA Training, Employee Rights, General Educational Diploma (GED), Pre-GED, English as a Second Language (ESL), Computer Literacy, and other courses designed to increase 'employability' in a local industry. Participants attend a Career Development & Skills Assessment workshop, which helps people recognize their strength, talents, and capacities; identify what works and doesn't work in a job for them. 265 SE Oak Street Suite A Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary, call for information. Email (503) 336-9895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides work clothing and employment support services. Work-Ready Clothing: Interview and work-ready clothing to help individuals transition into the workforce. Clients receive five pieces of work-ready clothing plus shoes, a coat, and accessories when they have a job interview, and five additional pieces of clothing when they get a job. Job Preparation: Provides job preparation classes for job seekers called Fast Track. Classes are currently online only. The regular cycle of four classes repeats monthly January-November. Additional services are available once Fast Track is completed. Employment Retention: The Professional Women's Group (PWG) is an ongoing group providing the opportunity for individuals to virtually meet with working women. Includes presentations and workshops to support life and career. The PWG meets regularly for programming with speakers from the community. Examples of program themes include financial education and planning, career development, the written and unwritten rules of the workplace, health and wellness, communication, and leadership skills. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Women and gender expansive individuals referred by a member agency Hours: Services: By appointment Job preparation: Thursday 10am-11:30am Email (503) 249-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration, school search and exploration, resources for standardized test preparation, admissions and scholarship assistance, and educational workshops. Virtual service only. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Scholarships must be a current or former NAYA participants and pursuing a post-secondary degree -All other services:Grades 9-12 and who self-identify as Native American Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 325-4803 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-8478 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OF JACKSON AND JOSEPHINE COUNTY NATIONAL FARMWORKER JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services to assist farmworkers (seasonal or migrant) in obtaining permanent and higher-paying employment opportunities. English as a second language, GED/High school equivalency test instruction, career services, classes, training, and on-the-job experiences, skills training and certifications for farmworkers, support with the job application and search process, career counseling, financial assistance for college, and education and career services for agricultural workers and their dependents. Depending on location and funding availability, specific classes, training opportunities, and financial assistance will vary. 820 S Central Avenue Suite A Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are an agricultural worker, the dependent, or spouse of an agricultural worker who meet federal income guidelines and are authorized to in the United States. Must be enrolled or in the process of enrolling at an accredited community college, vocational school/program, apprenticeship, or 4-year university. Also provides support in navigating the local job market, offering guidance on workers rights and workplace culture. 2410 SE 121st Avenue Suite #208 Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers -Low-income individuals and families residing in Oregon -Individuals with limited English proficiency -Families experiencing hardship, displacement, or housing instability -Youth, adults, and elders across all ages -BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, Persons of Color) -Rohingya and Myanmar Community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 489-5179 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SCHOOL FOR THE DEAF SCHOOL FOR THE DEAF Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a residential and day school for children who are deaf or hard of hearing. Also offers vocational and career education and an adult transition program for graduates. 999 Locust Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 3-21 who are eligible for special education in Oregon because they are Deaf or Hard of Hearing Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm Email (503) 400-6180 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE OPPORTUNITY CENTER AT 42ND AVENUE OPPORTUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration focusing on immigrant, refugee, and non-native English speakers. Offers skills-based training, workshops, and noncredit classes on healthcare, manufacturing, technology, and construction trades. Courses on financial literacy, basic computer workshops, and the Steps to Success program for Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) are available. 4299 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Varies by program or class Email (971) 722-2000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE WILLOW CREEK OPPORTUNITY CENTER OPPORTUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration focusing on immigrant, refugee, and non-native English speakers. Also, help businesses find skilled workers, grow their workforce, and access job training and pre-layoff support services. 6401 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 772-2300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE SE WORKS HAROLD WORKSOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources to help unemployed and underemployed people find jobs, connect with the right employers, get training for in-demand careers, and meet the requirements for unemployment benefits through a network of public and private partners.
